copy.heading: Build a home that's Yours
copy.attention: For streamers, artists, musicians, makers, and creators.
copy.tagline: Own your content. Build your community. Escape the algorithm goblins.

cta.text: Take the survey or get email updates.

visual.heading: You're Everywhere But Home
visual.body: Most creators are juggling platforms instead of building a foundation.

copy.heading: Followers Are Not Ownership
copy.subheading: Algorithms change. Platforms disappear. Accounts get suspended.
copy.body: 10,000 followers sounds impressive…but if you can't reach them tomorrow without asking a platform for permission, do you really own that audience?

platform.title: Problems
platform.heading: Platform Life

rows.words: on algorithms to be seen and grow
can change and wipe out your work

platform.bottom: You don't own your community. You're just borrowing access to it. And your content is feeding their growth as much as, if not more than yours.

creator.title: Solutions
creator.heading: Creator Home

rows.words: content is easy to find in one place
so your people can always find you
can be had and built upon

creator.bottom: The goal isn't replacing social media, its not another profile or link page. It's building something that's truly yours no matter which way the wind blows.

body: I've spent years watching streamers, artists, musicians, makers, and creators pour energy into platforms they don't control.
The tools I've found are often too limiting, too expensive, too complicated, or simply not designed with creators in mind.

vision.heading: I'm Building Something for Creators Like Us
vision.body: I'm researching a better way for creators to build websites that are flexible, affordable, and actually feel like their own.
vision.call: If that sounds interesting, I'd love your input.

survey.description: Let your voice be heard and shape the project

email.description: Get updates as things develop and first access
